titleStmt.title: Letter from James Langan to Sir Matthew Nathan, 6 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Langan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from James Langan (b. 1873), a collector in the Excise Branch of the Customs House, to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939). Langan writes that he will be forwarding the statements of 106 officials from the Dublin Excise Collection as to their whereabouts during the Easter Rising. Langan adds that due to the office remaining closed until Thursday and the restricted postal facilities he is missing ten statements which will be forwarded on later.Sir Matthew Nathan was the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/7720/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
CUSTOMS AND EXCISE OFFICE, Excise Branch, Custom House, Dublin. 6 May, 1916 Sir, In compliance with your letter dated the 1st. May, I beg to
forward statements made by 106 officials in Dublin (Excise)
Collection. As this office was not reopened until Thursday
the 4th. instant, I did not receive the above letter until that
day; and in view of the restricted Postal facilities, the time
between Thursday and Saturday at noon is scarcely sufficient for
getting statements from the whole of the officials who are scattered
all over the Dublin area from Clontarf to Rathmines, and Chapelizod
to Pembroke. I am consequently unable to send statements from
ten officials, but these will be forwarded later on. So far as I can ascertain I believe the statements now
forwarded give correct accounts of the movements of the persons
concerned. I beg to point out that there are two Collectors of Customs
and Excise with offices in this building, and I am informed that
the Collector of the Customs Branch has not been favoured with a
copy of the letter of the 1st. instant. As this may be due to
oversight I feel it right to mention the matter. I am, Sir,
Your obedient servant J. Langan
Collector (Excise) Sir Matthew Nathan Chief Secretary's Office Dublin Castle. I Send copy of letter of
1st inst. to Collector of C&Ex.,
Customs Branch.
II Chief Commissioner
8/5/16
